heading: Presumption of accessibility for statistical agencies and units
label: 3581 Presumption of accessibility for statistical agencies and units

children.heading: .—Accessibility of Data Assets
children.label: a .—Accessibility of Data Assets
children.text: The head of an agency shall, to the extent practicable, make any data asset maintained by the agency available, upon request, to any statistical agency or unit for purposes of developing evidence.
prohibits the sharing or intended use of such asset in a manner as to leave no discretion on the issue; or
if enacted after the date of the enactment of this section, specifically cites to this paragraph.
require the timely provision of data assets under subsection (a);
provide a list of statutes that exempt agencies from the requirement under subsection (a) pursuant to subsection (b)(1);

establish clear and consistent standards, to the extent possible, for complying with (commonly known as the “Privacy Act of 1974”) and any other applicable law requiring the protection and confidentiality of individually identifiable information; andsection 552a of title 5
require a transparent process for statistical agencies and units to request data assets from agencies and for agencies to respond to such requests.

children.heading: .—Rule of Construction
children.label: d .—Rule of Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed as altering existing intellectual property rights or the terms of any contract or other binding, written agreement.
